title: cuphea spraguei
intro: Cuphea spraguei, also known as Mexican Heather, is a flowering plant native to Mexico and Central America, characterized by its delicate pink or white flowers and shrub-like growth habit. Traditionally, Cuphea spraguei has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is also believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and acting as an antiseptic agent. Cuphea spraguei can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to leverage its medicinal and health benefits.
